Source: NCRP Year End Population (2000-2020), Annual Parole Survey (2000-2018)

Profile of People Released to Parole in 2020
Source: NCRP Releases (2020). Methodology: Found people with less than sentence length served and released unconditionally or conditionally. Removed releases labeled as Other release (including death, transfer, AWOL, escape).
Source: NCRP Year End Population

More Info on Parole Eligibility TBD
Ideas:

Is the PED missing? If so, is the data missing because they aren't parole eligible or because the data wasn't entered?
Source: NCRP Releases (2020)


Predicted Probabilities of Being Released Within 1 Year of Parole Eligibility in 2020

Source: NCRP Year End Population (2020). Methodology: Predicted probabilities are calculated using logistic regression --predicts the probabilities of being released within 1 year based on various predictor variables such as sex, race, admtype, offgeneral, and sentlgth.
More Info on Releases from Prison TBD
Ideas:

Who (demographics and criminal history) is being released at first eligibility?

How long after eligibility does release occur?

How does release vary by the person's demographic and criminal history characteristics?

What is the mean and median time between parole eligibility and release for all terms, terms with release bfore PED, and terms with release after PED?

Number/percentage of release types (conditional vs unconditional) from X year to Y year.

What is the mean and median time between parole eligibility and release for those released after the PED, by maximum sentence length? (When max sentence length is 1) up to 2 years, 2) 2 to 5 years, 3) 5 to 10 years, 4) 10 to 15 years, and 5) 15 years to less than life)

From 2000 to 2020, of the people eligible for release in that year who were released after their PED, what percentage are released in one year? Will do this by demographics and criminal history. For example, there will be a trend line for Black, Hispanic, and White individuals showing the number of people released within one year from 2000-2020.
Ideas:

The growth or decline of life sentences from X year to Y year (life without parole).

How different is actual time served compared to sentence length pronounced at court?

Bar graph showing the proportion of individuals who served at least 10 years by demographics and most serious sentenced offense.
